justification: All 7+ rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-9) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-6) included as invariant: true. Daily limit timezone ambiguity surfaced in low_confidence_areas ('Whether daily limits apply per calendar day, rolling 24h, or business day'). 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 52 is slightly below the 60-85 ideal range but is reasonably calibrated given the agent surfaced significant ambiguities around the partially-verified tier. The agent went beyond minimum requirements by splitting daily limits into separate rules for verified (BR-3) and partially-verified (BR-4) tiers with correct invariant: false classification, and adding support-agent unlock as a distinct rule (BR-8).

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified plus 2 additional valid rules (BR-4 for partially-verified limits, BR-8 for support-agent-only unlock)
Notification rule (BR-9) correctly classified as invariant: false with sound justification
Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant: true with strong regulatory justification
Timezone/reset ambiguity for daily limits surfaced in low_confidence_areas
13 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the 4 minimum
Excellent edge cases including exact-boundary $1,000 notification, mid-day tier upgrade, and pre-settlement reversal
Daily limit amounts (BR-3, BR-4) correctly marked invariant: false as soft config, while the existence of limits is enforced through the verification tier rules
Strong escalations identifying the partially-verified tier ambiguity and pre-settlement cancellation gap
Confidence level 52 is below the 60-85 rubric range — the partially-verified ambiguity drove it down more than warranted given the core rules are clearly defined
Correctly distinguishes regulatory invariants (KYC, sanctions, settlement finality) from operational/policy rules (notification threshold, specific dollar limits)
